steps: Cut paneer into 1-inch cubes and marinate in yogurt, turmeric, red chili powder, garam masala, ginger-garlic paste for 30 minutes.
Grill or pan-fry the marinated paneer until lightly charred on all sides. Set aside.
Heat oil in a heavy-bottomed pan. Add whole spices: 1 bay leaf, 2 cardamom pods, 1 cinnamon stick.
Add finely chopped onions and sauté until golden brown (8-10 minutes).
Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for 2 minutes until raw smell disappears.
Add pureed tomatoes, salt, and spices. Cook until oil separates (10 minutes).
Pour in heavy cream and simmer for 5 minutes to create the sauce.
Add grilled paneer, stir gently, and simmer for 5 more minutes.
Garnish with fresh cream, coriander leaves, and butter. Serve hot with naan or rice.
chef_tips: Use full-fat yogurt for the marinade to keep paneer tender.
Don't skip charring the paneer — it adds a smoky depth to the sauce.
Kashmiri red chili powder gives the signature colour without excessive heat.

steps: Marinate chicken in yogurt, spices, saffron-infused milk, fried onions, and mint for at least 2 hours.
Wash and soak basmati rice for 30 minutes.
Parboil rice with whole spices (bay leaf, star anise, cloves, cardamom) until 70% cooked. Drain.
In a heavy pot, spread the marinated chicken as the base layer.
Layer parboiled rice over the chicken evenly.
Drizzle saffron milk, fried onions, mint leaves, and ghee over the top layer.
Seal the pot tightly with dough or foil. Cook on high for 5 minutes, then low for 25 minutes (dum cooking).
Rest for 10 minutes before opening. Gently mix from the sides.
Serve with raita, mirchi salan, and lemon wedges.
chef_tips: The dum (steam) cooking is critical — do not open the lid during this stage.
Fried onions (birista) must be golden and crispy, not dark.
Saffron soaked in warm milk for 15 minutes gives the best colour and aroma.

steps: Warm 1 litre vegetable stock in a separate pot and keep at a gentle simmer.
In a wide pan, heat butter and olive oil. Sauté shallots and garlic until translucent.
Add Arborio rice and toast for 2 minutes, stirring constantly, until edges are translucent.
Add white wine and stir until absorbed.
Add warm stock one ladle at a time, stirring continuously. Add next ladle only when previous is absorbed.
Meanwhile, sauté mixed wild mushrooms in butter with thyme and garlic until golden.
After 20-25 minutes, rice should be al dente and creamy. Remove from heat.
Stir in cold butter and grated Parmesan vigorously (mantecatura) for creaminess.
Plate, top with sautéed mushrooms, shaved Parmesan, truffle oil, and fresh thyme.
chef_tips: Never rinse Arborio rice — you need the surface starch for creaminess.
Stock must be warm; cold stock shocks the rice and breaks the cooking.
The mantecatura (butter + cheese off heat) is what makes restaurant risotto impossibly creamy.

steps: Wash and soak 200g black lentils and 50g kidney beans overnight.
Pressure cook soaked lentils and beans with water, salt, and a pinch of baking soda for 6-7 whistles until completely soft.
In a separate pan, melt butter. Add cumin seeds, bay leaf, and dried chili.
Add finely chopped onions and cook until dark golden (15 minutes).
Add ginger-garlic paste, cook 3 minutes. Add tomato puree and spices, cook until oil separates.
Add the cooked lentils to the tomato base. Mix well and simmer on very low heat.
For authentic flavour, simmer for 4-6 hours, stirring occasionally and adding water as needed.
Add heavy cream in the last 30 minutes. Adjust salt.
Finish with a knob of butter and fresh coriander. Serve with naan or rice.
chef_tips: The longer it simmers, the better — overnight slow cooking is ideal.
A charcoal smoking technique (dhungar) adds authentic dhaba-style smoky flavour.
Use Amul butter for the most authentic taste.

steps: Preheat oven to 220°C (425°F). Grease four ramekins with butter and dust with cocoa powder.
Melt 200g dark chocolate (70% cocoa) and 150g butter together in a double boiler. Cool slightly.
Whisk 4 eggs and 4 yolks with 100g sugar until pale and thick.
Fold the chocolate mixture into the egg mixture gently.
Sift in 50g plain flour and fold until just combined — do not overmix.
Divide batter equally into prepared ramekins. Can be refrigerated at this point for up to 24 hours.
Bake for 10-12 minutes until edges are set but centre still jiggles slightly.
Rest for 1 minute, then invert onto serving plates.
Serve immediately with vanilla ice cream and fresh berries.
chef_tips: The bake time is crucial — 10 min for very runny, 12 min for a fudgy centre.
Good quality chocolate makes all the difference — use 70%+ cocoa.
Ramekins can be prepped hours ahead and refrigerated; bake straight from the fridge, adding 1 minute.
